zh.title: 美元/日元技术分析：高位震荡偏弱整理
zh.content:
当前USDJPY在161.7附近窄幅整理，短线动能减弱，整体仍处高位震荡结构，呈现涨后盘整格局，方向不明但偏弱整理延续

SMA20位于161.59附近，价格略高于均线，短期仍保持多头上方优势，但力度有限；MACD维持小幅正值，动能偏弱；RSI缺失，需谨慎判断超买情况

下方初步支撑位于161.58及161.50区域，上方阻力集中在161.74前高与162.00整数关口，短线区间压缩明显

操作上建议以区间交易为主，回踩161.60附近可轻仓做多，跌破161.50止损，上看161.74及162.00，若放量跌破则转为空头思路

en.title: US Dollar vs Japanese Yen Technical Analysis
en.content:
USD/JPY continues to trade within a broader bullish structure, though short-term momentum has begun to flatten as price hovers around 161.69. The pair has shown limited downside despite a slight 24-hour decline, indicating persistent underlying demand. Recent price action suggests a consolidation phase near elevated levels rather than a decisive reversal. Buyers remain active on dips, but upward continuation lacks strong impulsive expansion at the moment.

Price is trading marginally above the 20-period SMA at 161.58597, reinforcing a mild bullish bias in the short term. The absence of RSI limits momentum clarity, but MACD shows a slightly positive reading at 0.0239, suggesting weak yet constructive bullish momentum. However, the lack of a confirmed signal line implies uncertainty in trend strength. Overall, indicators point to consolidation with a slight upward tilt.

Key support is located near the 20-period SMA at 161.58, followed by a stronger demand zone around 161.50, which has acted as a short-term floor. On the upside, immediate resistance is observed near the recent high at 161.74, with further barriers around 161.80 and the psychological 162.00 level. A sustained break above resistance could open the path toward continuation of the broader uptrend.

Trading conditions favor a range-bound-to-bullish approach. Buying dips near 161.50–161.58 with tight risk control below 161.40 may offer favorable reward potential, targeting 161.80 and 162.00 on upside continuation. Aggressive traders may wait for a confirmed breakout above 161.80 before adding long exposure. Short positions remain countertrend and should be limited to rejection signals near resistance, given the prevailing upward structural bias. Overall market sentiment remains constructive despite short-term hesitation.
